title: GIS Foundation at Wuhan University
dek: Gengchen Mai began his academic journey at Wuhan University, earning a B.S. in Geographic Information System with exceptional academic distinction.
narrative: From September 2011 to June 2015, Gengchen Mai studied at Wuhan University's School of Resource and Environmental Sciences, one of China's premier GIS programs. His undergraduate thesis focused on 'Tea Plantation Expansion in Southeast of China: Process, Driving Forces and Ecological Effect' under Prof. Shiliang Su.

Mai's exceptional academic performance earned him the Chinese National Scholarship for three consecutive years (2011-2012, 2012-2013, 2013-2014), a prestigious honor recognizing top students. Upon graduation, he was named one of only 11 Outstanding Undergraduate Students out of over 8,000 graduates from Wuhan University in 2015.
This foundational education in Geographic Information Science set the stage for Mai's future research in spatially explicit AI and geospatial data science.
proofPoints: Chinese National Scholarship recipient for three consecutive years (2011-2014)
Outstanding Undergraduate Student of Wuhan University (11 out of 8000+ students)
Undergraduate thesis on tea plantation expansion and ecological effects
mapCaption: B.S. in Geographic Information System from Wuhan University, China (2011-2015).

title: Graduate Training at UC Santa Barbara
dek: M.A. and Ph.D. in Geography from UCSB, specializing in Cartography, GIS, and Cognitive Science, with distinguished research and industry experience.
narrative: From September 2015 to June 2021, Gengchen Mai pursued graduate studies at UC Santa Barbara's Department of Geography under the mentorship of Prof. Krzysztof Janowicz. He completed his M.A. in Cartography and Geographic Information Science (2015-2017) with a thesis on 'ADCN: An Anisotropic Density-Based Clustering Algorithm for Discovering Spatial Point Patterns with Noise.'

His doctoral research (2017-2021) at the STKO Lab and UCSB Spatial Center focused on 'Geographic Question Answering with Spatially-Explicit Machine Learning Models,' with an emphasis in Cognitive Science. This groundbreaking work earned him multiple prestigious awards: the Jack and Laura Dangermond Graduate Fellowship, AAG 2021 Dissertation Research Grant, AAG 2022 William L. Garrison Award for Best Dissertation in Computational Geography (awarded biennially), and the AAG 2023 J. Warren Nystrom Award.
During his graduate studies, Mai also spent time at the National Center for Ecological Analysis & Synthesis (NCEAS) from January to April 2016, further broadening his research experience.

Mai completed strategic internships at major tech companies, bridging academic research with industry applications: Software Development at Esri (Summer 2017) working on Elasticsearch and ML/NLP; Machine Learning & NLP Research at SayMosaic Inc. (Summer 2018) focusing on Deep Reinforcement Learning and Q&A systems; Cartographic Engineer at Apple Maps (Summer 2019) developing Graph Neural Networks for map generalization; and two AI Resident positions at Google X (Summer and Fall 2020) working on Time Series Forecasting and Causal Discovery/Inference.
proofPoints: Ph.D. dissertation: Geographic Question Answering with Spatially-Explicit Machine Learning Models
Jack and Laura Dangermond Graduate Fellowship recipient
AAG 2022 William L. Garrison Award for Best Dissertation in Computational Geography
AAG 2023 J. Warren Nystrom Award
Research at STKO Lab, UCSB Spatial Center, and NCEAS
Industry internships at Esri, SayMosaic, Apple Maps, and Google X
mapCaption: Graduate studies at UC Santa Barbara (2015-2021), earning M.A. and Ph.D. in Geography.

title: Postdoctoral Research at Stanford AI Lab
dek: Postdoctoral Scholar at Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory and Stanford Sustainability and AI Lab, working under Prof. Stefano Ermon.
narrative: From August 2021 to August 2022, Gengchen Mai served as a Postdoctoral Scholar at Stanford University's School of Engineering, Department of Computer Science. He worked at both the Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (SAIL) and the Stanford Sustainability and Artificial Intelligence Lab under the mentorship of Prof. Stefano Ermon.

This postdoctoral position represented a critical transition from pure geography to the intersection of AI and spatial data science, allowing Mai to deepen his expertise in machine learning while maintaining his geographic focus. The experience at one of the world's leading AI research institutions positioned Mai at the forefront of GeoAI research and prepared him for his subsequent faculty positions.
proofPoints: Postdoctoral Scholar at Stanford AI Lab (SAIL)
Research at Stanford Sustainability and AI Lab
Mentored by Prof. Stefano Ermon
mapCaption: Postdoctoral research at Stanford AI Lab (2021-2022), bridging geography and artificial intelligence.

title: Building a Research Program at University of Georgia
dek: Tenure-Track Assistant Professor at UGA Geography, founding the SEAI Lab and establishing cross-disciplinary collaborations.
narrative: From August 2022 to May 2024, Gengchen Mai served as Tenure-Track Assistant Professor in the Department of Geography at the University of Georgia's Franklin College of Arts and Sciences. He founded and directed the Spatially Explicit Artificial Intelligence (SEAI) Lab at UGA, establishing a research program focused on spatially explicit AI, geographic knowledge graphs, and geo-foundation models.

Mai built extensive cross-disciplinary connections, serving as Affiliated Professor with both the Department of Computer Science (School of Computing) and the UGA Institute for Artificial Intelligence. He was also a member of the UGA Environmental Artificial Intelligence Faculty Cluster and the Institute for Integrative Precision Agriculture (IIPA).

His research achievements during this period were recognized with the prestigious 2022 AAG William L. Garrison Award for Best Dissertation in Computational Geography (awarded biennially) and the 2023 AAG J. Warren Nystrom Award for his work on representation learning of polygonal geometries. These honors established Mai as a leading researcher in GeoAI while he trained graduate students and published influential work in the field.
proofPoints: Founded and directed SEAI Lab at UGA
2022 AAG William L. Garrison Award for Best Dissertation in Computational Geography
2023 AAG J. Warren Nystrom Award
Affiliated Professor with Computer Science and AI Institute
Member of Environmental AI Faculty Cluster and IIPA
mapCaption: Tenure-Track Assistant Professor at University of Georgia (2022-2024), establishing GeoAI research program.

title: Leading GeoAI Research at UT Austin
dek: Tenure-Track Assistant Professor at UT Austin Geography, directing the SEAI Lab and serving as Visiting Faculty at Google Research.
narrative: In June 2024, Gengchen Mai joined the University of Texas at Austin as Tenure-Track Assistant Professor in the Department of Geography and the Environment, College of Liberal Arts. He founded and directs the Spatially Explicit Artificial Intelligence (SEAI) Lab at UT Austin, focusing on GeoAI, geo-foundation models, and geographic knowledge graphs.

While establishing his new research program at UT Austin, Mai maintains strong connections to UGA, serving as Adjunct Professor in the Department of Geography and Faculty Fellow at the UGA Institute for Artificial Intelligence.

Mai's research addresses critical challenges in trustworthy AI, including geographic bias in AI models. In 2025, he was awarded a $300,000 NSF grant to develop frameworks for measuring and mitigating geographic bias in both task-specific geospatial AI models and foundation models.

Since June 2025, Mai has also served as a Visiting Faculty Researcher at Google Research in Mountain View, CA, bridging academic research with industry applications in GeoAI and foundation models. This dual role allows him to advance both theoretical research and practical applications of spatially explicit artificial intelligence.
proofPoints: Founded and directs SEAI Lab at UT Austin
Adjunct Professor at UGA Geography and Faculty Fellow at UGA AI Institute
$300,000 NSF grant for geographic bias research (2025)
Visiting Faculty Researcher at Google Research (2025-present)
mapCaption: Current position at UT Austin (2024-present), leading GeoAI research and the SEAI Lab.
